caption:
All Gyms Closed Friday, 1/30

We stand with the people of Minneapolis and with our employees. 

We are closing as part of the national general strike in opposition to ICE and the violence, fear, and harm they continue to inflict on immigrant communities and those who stand with them. 

While the gyms are closed, all hourly employees who were scheduled to work will be paid.

We do not make this decision lightly, but we no longer feel it is acceptable to remain silent in the face of these actions. 

#touchstoneclimbing #laclimbing #bayareaclimbing #sacramentoclimbing #fresnoclimbing

caption:
⚡️ Woman Up 2026 ⚡️
First ROPED Woman Up competition ever
Calling📢 all women and non-binary routesetters 

As the largest women’s and non-binary rock-climbing competition on this, or any side of the Sierra, Woman Up is set by women and non-binary setters, for women and non-binary climbers. 

Held at @class5gym in Torrance on Aug 22-23, this year is our first-ever roped-climbing Woman Up, and our routesetter’s application is now officially open 🙌.

This is an amazing opportunity to both use and up-level your routesetting skillz 😎. Here are the criteria we use during our selection process:
⚡️Women and non-binary
⚡️Skill & Experience
⚡️Mentorship & Growth Potential

Skill & experience usually mean USAC levels and competition experience (as a competitor or routesetter). But this is balanced by our desire to provide avenues for growth and mentorship.

Learn more and apply at the link 🔗 in our bio. 

#womanup2025 #womanup #touchstoneclimbing #laclimbing #class5climbing
author: touchstoneclimbing

caption:
🤩 Gym to Crag Across LA 🤩

Last week, we wrapped up three Gym to Crag events at @cliffsofid, @class5gym, and @thepostclimbing. The turnout was incredible—huge crowds, great energy ⚡️, and thoughtful conversations all around.

Each event included:

 💜 The @bishopclimbingrangers
 💜 An @accessfund Climbing Steward from Red Rock
 💜 A @joshuatreenps National Park Ranger
 💜 @joshuatreefriends

From tabling to casual one-on-one chats to interactive Gym to Crag trivia, the nights were complete with drinks, snacks, prizes, insightful questions 🙋‍♀️, participatory learning, and meaningful connections. 

Considering the importance of stewardship and responsible recreation, we covered climbing history, ecology, geology, and Leave No Trace principles specific to Bishop, Joshua Tree, and Red Rock. Many participants shared that they learned a ton about outdoor climbing ethics, land management, and access issues. 

With indoor climbing continuing to be the fastest-growing entry point into the sport, events like these play a key role in helping climbers feel informed, prepared, and excited to take their climbing outside responsibly.

Thanks to everyone who came out 🙌—we’re stoked to keep building these connections from gym to crag.

#laclimbing #class5climbing #cliffsofid #thepostclimbing #touchstoneclimbing

caption:
👋 Goodbye 2025 👋

Let’s be real—2025 was a year that included all the things ☹️🤣😻🥳

But looking back 👀, we’re filled with gratitude for all we achieved together 💜

From surpassing 100,000 free showers 🛀 provided to those in need to countless beautiful moments at competitions, parties, climbing sessions, Affinity Groups, film fests 📽️, and more, it was a big year for the Touchstone Community. 

Thank you 🙏 to everyone who allowed us to be a part of their journey—and in turn became a part of ours. And a big shoutout 🙌 to @outpostcinema for capturing the magic of it! 

Here’s to co-creating a 2026 filled with more laughter, joy, expansion, integration, hope, and good times for all. 

Let’s keep climbing. 

📽️: @outpostcinema

#touchstoneclimbing #laclimbing #bayareaclimbing #fresnoclimbing #sacramentoclimbing goodbye2025 hello2026

caption:
🎬 Girl Climber 🎬

We’re thrilled to be hosting screenings of “Girl Climber” 🙌 

Having summited Everest, 8000-meter peaks, and dominated the competition circuit, Emily Harrington 💪 seeks to cement her professional-rock-climbing 🧗‍♀️ legacy in this male-dominated world with a 24-hour ascent of Yosemite’s El Capitan. After a near-fatal fall, she must reckon with what she’s willing to risk. 

Equal parts gripping survival story 😬 and intimate portrait, Girl Climber is about more than breaking records; it’s about breaking barriers 💥. Join the journey as this “Girl Climber” proves she’s one of the best—period.  

Here are the deets:

caption:
Touchstone Speed Series 🏁

Think you’re fast on the wall? Prove it.

Join us for the Touchstone Speed Series at Pac Pipe— a three-part, low-pressure sprint through the ranks of speed climbing. Whether it’s your first time or you’re chasing PRs, these nights are all about fun, community, and a little friendly competition.

🔥 Event 1 (Dec 14): Open Sprint Night – Anyone can try. Prizes for time tiers.

⚡ Event 2 (Feb 15): Bracket Qualifiers – Head-to-head showdowns. March Madness style.

🏆 Event 3 (April 19): Knockout Finals – Hype, crowd energy, and bragging rights.

Free for members, $30 for non-members.
Welcome to anyone interested in trying speed climbing.

Show up, climb fast, and see where you stack up. 🏁

caption:
🤯 Showers by Touchstone Hits 100k 🤯

Our Showers by Touchstone program, run out of @pipeworksclimbing parking lot, offers tangible support to community members experiencing homelessness in Sacramento. And we’re happy 💜 to say we’ve hit (and passed) 100,000 showers. 

For those experiencing homelessness, a fresh shower and change of clothes is a huge reprieve. In addition to free showers 🚿, Showers by Touchstone provides:

 💧 Clean water
 🥪Meals
 👖 Clothing
 ❤️‍🩹 Mobile medicine
 🪥Other essentials

It’s one of the ways we’re proud to give back to our communities. From community fridges to holiday clothing drives and beyond, our gym communities support the broader communities they’re a part of throughout the year. Chat with the front desk or your gym’s manager to get involved. 

Let’s keep climbing. 

#sacramentoclimbing #pipeworksclimbing #togetherwerise #touchstoneclimbing

caption:
🎥 Negus in Nature Doc Drop 🎥

In this age of chaos, how can we support those who are working for the reckoning that leads to reconciliation?

Negus in Nature was born from this question—in the middle of the pandemic, in the heart of Oakland, Ca.  

Returning to nature 🌲, to the body and breath 😮‍💨, to being seen in community. From the crag 🪨 to the redwoods to the ocean 🌊 to the snow 🧊, @negusinnature is creating spaces for those from historically marginalized communities to access the restorative spaces of the natural world. 

We’re so proud 🙌 of the work they do and so humbled to be a part of it. 🐋 

After touring film festivals (and some of our gyms!), the full doc is now available on YouTube. Just follow the link 🔗 in our bio to watch it all.

Directed by: @michael_alfuso
Produced by: @kinglangbang
Executive Producer: @touchstoneclimbing
Film: @jonathan_jp_park @maxbuschini @michael_alfuso
Edited by: @ericjosephl @kford_525 @michael_alfuso

#Negusinnature #touchstoneclimbing #bayareaclimbing #californiaclimbing #coloradoclimbing #getoutthere #getoutside #outpostcinema

caption:
🙌 Woman Up 2025 🙌

We started Woman Up in 2017 to support women 🧗‍♀️ as they carved out their own space in this sport and industry. A lot has changed since then. What hasn’t changed is how much we love 💜 this event. 

Whether swapping beta during the Citizens comp, checking out local makers, eating, dancing, and drinking with friends,  hearing Emily Seelenfreund’s keynote, or puzzling over the problems with the Open finalists (that rainbow volume problem 🤯), this event always blows our minds, opens our hearts, and brings us so much joy. 

A huge thank you 🙏  to everyone who showed up. From Friday’s opening party 🍻 to Sunday’s clinics 👷, it was a weekend to remember. We love our community. Thanks for making space for us all. Stay tuned for more photos from the weekend!

📸: @oldmanmoon

#touchstoneclimbing #keepclimbing #womanup2025

caption:
🧗‍♀️Staying Alive Clinics 🧗‍♀️

The Yosemite Climbing Rangers (@climber_stewards), @FOYOSAR, and @yosemiteclimbingassociation just wrapped up an awesome trip to SoCal for the “Staying Alive” climbing clinics at @ThePostClimbing and @CliffsofId.

They covered core skills like: 

  💥Cleaning anchors
  💥Lowering from single-pitch routes
  💥Rappelling

From first-timers to seasoned climbers, a wide range of participants joined. Each clinic wrapped with a presentation on helicopter 🚁rescues and a short film 📽️ celebrating the vertical wilderness of Yosemite National Park.

We were so stoked 💜 to partner with all of them to help expand best practices and safety in the wilderness, which has given us so much.

If you’re looking to learn the skills needed to stay safe in the outdoors and on the big walls, be sure to follow the above to keep up to date on trainings in your area. 

#touchstoneclimbing #staysafe #climbingbestpractices #climbingsafety #laclimbing

caption:
🏞️2 huge Sierra days w/ Touchstone athlete: Colin Wills 🏞️ Inspiration level: off the charts. 📈 

These kinds of days, only two weeks apart, remind us what’s possible with a deep love for the mountains. Keep reading for a breakdown of each of these mega days.💪

🏔️ July 16: Russell, Whitney, Muir (12:10, car-to-car)
“Started from Whitney Portal at 5:08am. Tagged Russell via the east ridge—3rd class and mellow. Traversed down and around to Whitney’s East Buttress. Swapped shoes, ditched poles, and started climbing at 11:36am. Routefinding was straightforward with a few 5.6-5.7 moves. Summited Whitney at 12:50pm, hung out with hikers asking: “Did you just free climb up here?” Topped Muir by 2:15pm via 4th class blocks, then ran the trail back to the car by 5:18pm.
18.6 mi / 8,068’ / 27,470 steps.”

🏔️ July 28: Thunderbolt to Sill (13:57, car-to-car)
“Started from South Lake at 5:48am. Hiked up Bishop Pass and made good time to Thunderbolt Col. Tagged Thunderbolt, then linked Starlight, North Pal, Polemonium, and Sill. Route felt mostly low-to-mid 5th with some spicy exposed sections—especially between Thunderbolt and Starlight. Used a 30m rope to rappel in a few places only to rappel, mostly downclimbed or scrambled. Only climbed in trail runners; brought climbing shoes but didn’t use them. Got back to the car at 7:45pm.
21.8 mi / 7,776’ / 32,106 steps.”

Gear for both: 👟 trail runners, 👕 sun hoody, 💧vest w/ two 500mL flasks + 1.5L bottle, ⌚️Garmin InReach, 🥙 food for the day, 🪢 30m static rope.

🤯 Impressive couple of days @cw.climbs!

#touchstoneclimbing #KeepClimbing #californiaclimbing

caption:
🤯 Touchstone turns 30 🤯
Let’s celebrate this August

Thirty years ago, we opened Mission Cliffs 🧗‍♀️ in San Francisco. We had no clue we’d end up where we are today, but we’re thankful 🙏 for every minute.

From hard times to celebratory moments, it’s always been about two things:
 💥 Climbing 
 💥 Community 

Whether you joined us at Mission Cliffs 30 years ago or at Hyperion last week, we’re so glad you’re here. And we hope you’ll join us in celebrating 🍾 this milestone.

Throughout August, we’ll be hosting rolling celebrations at all our gyms, complete with some fun swag 😎 for our members. Keep your eyes peeled 👀 for a celebration rolling through near you. 

Thanks for being awesome 🙌. We couldn’t do this (and wouldn’t want to!) without you. 

#touchstoneclimbingturns30 #touchstoneclimbing

caption:
🥈Another World Cup Silver🥈

Emily Seelenfreund is a Touchstone Athlete ⚡️ and three-time Para-national champion 💪 in her category. She’s also won three silver 🥈 and one bronze 🥉 at IFSC World Cups. 

‘Cause that’s, apparently, not enough, @emilyseelenfreund is also the co-founder of the national adaptive rock-climbing group @paracliffhangers . In 2022, she received a Climbing Advocate Award 🏆 from the Access Fund.

When asked about her latest achievement on the world athletic stage, she had this to say:

“This is my fifth year competing at IFSC Paraclimbing Events and each year I’ve heard that the Innsbruck World Cup is a must-attend. This year the stars finally aligned and I was able to make the trip to compete in Austria! Between the incredible facility and outdoor comp wall at  Kletterzentrum Innsbruck, the packed crowd, and the gorgeous mountain scenery it was definitely a competition to remember.

“Qualifiers started out a little rocky. On my first route, the double-belay system (used at most paraclimbing competitions to allow climbers to steep overhangs on top rope without big swings) got tangled, creating rope drag and leading to a big whip. A technical incident was called, and I got to climb the route again, but my confidence was definitely shaken. Route two, a technical, leg-heavy climb, was not in my wheelhouse. Still, I managed to sneak into finals in fourth position, earning the chance to climb the epic finals route, which featured huge, eye-catching volumes that competitors had to traverse across.

“I’m stoked to have come away with a silver medal and am already back to training hard for the World Championships, coming up in Seoul in less than three months!”

A huge congrats 🥳🙌🍾 for this amazing accomplishment! 

#touchstoneclimbing #paraclimbing #worldcup #touchstoneathlete

author: touchstoneclimbing

caption:
Fresh off her third place win at the Paraclimbing World Cup in Salt Lake City recently, Touchstone athlete Ellie Rubin (@ellrubin) gives us a trip report about her experience and what it takes to make podium at an international competition:

“I had a great time at the first Para World Cup of the season in Salt Lake City. It’s always fun to start the season off with a competition on home turf. This year I kept my training mostly focused on bouldering and finger strength, while also setting aside some time for mental training. I was coming off a stressful Nationals and really wanted to enjoy competing at SLC, so I worked on some mindfulness techniques with my coaches and nutritionist. And it really paid off because I climbed the best I’d ever done in a comp, especially mentally, and came away with third place. I’m glad to start the season off so well because I won’t be doing the next WC in Innsbruck. I’ll instead be going straight to World Champs in South Korea in September so I’m looking forward to having a long period to train and get ready. Thanks to Touchstone for providing all the support and incredible facilities to train in to chase my goals!”

📸: @gajdaphotography

#climbing #KeepClimbing #touchstone #competition

caption:
👷‍♀️ How to Build A Gym 👷‍♂️

Ever wondered 🤔 what goes into making the spaces you climb, work out, do yoga, and vibe in? The answer is a lot. 

Is it a rope or bouldering-only gym? Will we be serving families, students, or young professionals? Answering these is just the start. 

From city regulations ❌ and politics to unforeseen hiccups in supply chains, a lot can happen between the time a lease is signed ✍️ and the doors are opened to you.

We thought it would be fun to capture 🤳 some of it—wall design, location, construction, demographics, and more—while we built out Class 5 in Torrance. Get a behind-the-scenes look at the process behind the magic🪄. 

Follow the link in our bio 🧬 to watch the whole video.

#class5 #touchstoneclimbing #laclimbing #indoorclimbing
